Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Mar-29-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Dept. 514, Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: A. Mok, CSR: Mitch Lyon, CSR#68408.
Michael D. Levinson, Esq., appearing for Plaintiffs, HERB ROMINE and PATRICIA ROMINE.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-
up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY.
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against
Defaulted Defendant as follows:
IT IS ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED that the plaintiffs shall recover damages from
defendant MARINE ENGINEERING AND SUPPLY COMPANY, in the amount of:

As to Plaintiff HERB ROMINE, $441,992.39 in Economic Damages,
AND $2,000,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,

and as to Plaintiff PATRICIA ROMINE, $500,000.00 in Non-Economic Damages,
Total Judgment of $2,941,992.39.

The Clerk is ordered to enter the judgment.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ferlin P. Ruiz, Esq., of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST LLP (415-617-2222), appearing for defendant / moving party Carrier Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Herb Romine, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Carrier Corporation's motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. There is a triable issue of material fact as to whether Plaintiff Herb Romine knew or should have known of the hazards of exposure to asbestos caused by working with Carrier's products and whether he was, therefore, a sophisticated user under Johnson v. American Standard (2008) 43 Cal.4th 56. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tamara Willat, CSR #4609 = (503/TLJ)
